hero_subtitle: SFTY helps regulators connect Travel Rule data, wallet risk signals, and transaction monitoring into evidence-ready workflows for structured suspicious activity review and escalation.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data

problem_title: Suspicious activity alerts lack clear escalation
problem_description: Regulators struggle to detect and escalate suspicious transactions when Travel Rule data and wallet risk signals remain disconnected. This creates gaps in evidence, slows investigative workflows and weakens supervisory response across digital asset markets.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY integrates Travel Rule data, wallet risk alerts and KYT signals into a unified case management workflow that supports evidence collection, escalation and regulatory review for suspicious activity.
workflow_title: From wallet risk to supervisory action
workflow_description: SFTY links wallet and transaction risk signals to supervisory alerts, case creation, evidence collection and reporting workflows for structured suspicious activity review.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. One supervisory layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented wallet, transaction, Travel Rule, and KYT data into evidence-ready cases and reports for structured suspicious activity supervision.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY delivers a secure, auditable framework for regulators requiring controlled access, evidence-grade logging and procurement-safe deployment in sensitive government environments.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how suspicious activity links to Travel Rule data
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how suspicious transactions trigger supervisory case workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging supports suspicious activity reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe provider orchestration for managing Travel Rule exceptions
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for regulators supervising suspicious activity?
faq_1_answer: SFTY helps regulators connect Travel Rule, KYT signals, transaction data and entity profiles into structured supervisory workflows to detect, review and escalate suspicious digital asset activity.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle suspicious case workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures suspicious signals from wallet activity, Travel Rule, alerts and providers into cases with clear roles, review notes, escalations and decision logs for consistent supervisory action.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve evidence for investigations?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves detailed case history, evidence records, decision logs and supervisory actions to support audit readiness and defensible review of suspicious activity investigations.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing regulatory tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY operates as a neutral layer connecting multiple providers and government systems, enabling integration while maintaining secure access governance and audit logs.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace analytics or compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY supplements existing analytics and compliance providers by organizing their outputs into a connected, evidence-grade supervisory platform without replacing them.
final_title: Structure suspicious activity into reviewable cases
final_description: See how SFTY supports connecting Travel Rule data, wallet risk signals and transaction monitoring into evidence-grade supervisory workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
